text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Okonkwo is a 64-year-old female with a known history of stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma of the right kidney, status post right radical nephrectomy with regional lymph node dissection. She completed four induction cycles of ipilimumab/nivolumab followed by nivolumab maintenance, with radiographic progressive disease documented at approximately nine months on first-line immunotherapy. She was subsequently transitioned to cabozantinib 60 mg orally daily (later dose-reduced to 40 mg daily for palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia) approximately three months ago. This examination is obtained for restaging to assess treatment response. The patient has documented chronic kidney disease with a baseline creatinine of approximately 1.55 mg/dL; contrast was administered with nephrology awareness and adequate pre-hydration.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 80 mL of intravenous iohexol (Omnipaque 350) administered via a right antecubital peripheral IV at 2.5 mL/sec. Arterial, portal venous, and delayed phase acquisitions were obtained through the abdomen and pelvis. Chest was imaged in the portal venous phase. Multiplanar reconstructions were generated in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. Images are compared to the prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis obtained approximately three months ago at the time of documented progression on immunotherapy, as well as the CT obtained at the time of initial diagnosis.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally. There has been interval improvement in the previously documented bilateral pulmonary nodules. The dominant nodule in the right lower lobe, which previously measured 2.4 cm in greatest axial dimension, has decreased appreciably in size and now measures approximately 1.9 cm in greatest axial dimension. The lesion remains well-circumscribed with slightly irregular margins, consistent with its known metastatic etiology. No internal cavitation, calcification, or pleural tethering is identified. The right upper lobe nodule, previously measuring 1.2 cm, has similarly decreased and now measures approximately 0.9 cm; this lesion is rounded and solid in morphology. In the left lower lobe, the previously identified nodule has decreased from 1.5 cm to approximately 1.2 cm; it retains a geographic, mildly lobulated contour. The cluster of bilateral small pulmonary nodules seen at the time of prior progression appear stable to mildly decreased in conspicuity; several of the previously described sub-centimeter nodules measuring up to 4 mm bilaterally are at the lower limits of reliable measurement and do not appear definitively enlarged. No new pulmonary nodules are identified to the limits of CT resolution. There is no endobronchial lesion, lobar atelectasis, or pneumonia. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax is identified. The central airways remain clear.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The mediastinal and hilar contours appear unremarkable. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy is identified. The trachea is midline. The thyroid gland is not fully imaged but the visualized inferior poles appear symmetric without gross nodularity.

Heart and Pericardium:
The cardiac silhouette is normal in size. No pericardial effusion is seen. The visualized coronary arteries show mild calcification in the left anterior descending distribution, unchanged.

Bony Thorax:
The ribs, sternum, and thoracic vertebral bodies are intact without lytic or sclerotic lesion suspicious for osseous metastatic involvement. Mild degenerative changes are present at multiple thoracic vertebral levels.

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size and demonstrates homogeneous parenchymal enhancement without focal lesion, mass, or abnormal hypervascular or hypovascular area suspicious for metastatic disease. No hepatic cysts or hemangiomas are identified. The hepatic veins and portal venous system are patent. No biliary ductal dilation.

Spleen:
The spleen is normal in size and morphology, with homogeneous enhancement. No splenic lesion.

Pancreas:
The pancreas enhances homogeneously without ductal dilation or focal mass.

Gallbladder and Biliary System:
The gallbladder contains a small dependent calculus measuring approximately 5 mm without evidence of acute cholecystitis. The common bile duct is not dilated.

Adrenal Glands:
Both adrenal glands are within normal limits without nodule or mass. No adrenal metastatic lesion is identified. The left adrenal gland has a normal limb thickness bilaterally.

Kidneys:
The right renal fossa demonstrates expected postsurgical changes following right radical nephrectomy, with soft tissue stranding and clip artifact at the operative bed. No local tumor recurrence is identified in the nephrectomy bed on this examination, though assessment is somewhat limited by postsurgical changes and streak artifact from surgical clips. The left kidney is present and demonstrates normal size, morphology, and cortical enhancement. No hydronephrosis or perinephric collection on the left. The left renal parenchyma appears intact without focal lesion.

Retroperitoneum:
No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy is identified above the aortic bifurcation. The aorta is normal in caliber. The right para-aortic and aortocaval regions, previously the site of lymph node dissection, demonstrate expected postoperative soft tissue changes without clearly enlarged nodal masses. No new retroperitoneal nodal disease.

Other Abdominal Structures:
No free fluid or free air within the abdomen. The small and large bowel loops appear normal in caliber and configuration without wall thickening, obstruction, or pneumatosis.

PELVIS:

Lymph Nodes:
The right pelvic sidewall lymph node, previously measuring 1.9 cm in short axis at the time of progression and since representing a new site of nodal involvement identified during first-line immunotherapy, has decreased to approximately 1.4 cm in short axis on this examination. The node retains a rounded rather than ovoid morphology, and its short-axis measurement remains above the threshold expected for a reactive node. Compared to the prior study, this represents a meaningful decrease in size. The right external iliac lymph node, previously measuring 1.4 cm and then 1.4 cm at most recent imaging, now measures approximately 1.1 cm in short axis. This node has also decreased appreciably in size and is approaching an indeterminate size range, though it remains mildly enlarged. No new pelvic sidewall, external iliac, obturator, or inguinal lymphadenopathy is identified.

Bladder and Reproductive Organs:
The urinary bladder appears unremarkable with smooth walls and no intraluminal filling defect. The uterus and bilateral adnexa are within normal limits without pelvic mass.

Bony Pelvis:
The pelvic bones are intact without lytic or blastic lesion to suggest osseous metastatic involvement. No pathologic fracture. Mild degenerative joint changes at both sacroiliac joints and hip joints.

Soft Tissues:
No soft tissue mass or subcutaneous lesion in the imaged soft tissues.

IMPRESSION:

1. Meaningful decrease in size of the dominant right lower lobe pulmonary metastasis (now approximately 1.9 cm, previously 2.4 cm) as well as the right upper lobe and left lower lobe pulmonary nodules, all showing reduction from the pre-cabozantinib baseline. The bilateral small pulmonary nodules are stable to decreased. The overall pulmonary metastatic burden has notably diminished compared to the prior examination documenting progressive disease on immunotherapy.

2. Decrease in size of the right pelvic sidewall lymph node (now approximately 1.4 cm, previously 1.9 cm) and the right external iliac lymph node (now approximately 1.1 cm, previously 1.4 cm), consistent with a favorable nodal response to cabozantinib.

3. No new sites of metastatic disease identified. No hepatic, adrenal, or osseous metastatic lesions. No evidence of local recurrence in the right nephrectomy bed, though assessment is limited by postsurgical changes.

4. Stable postoperative changes in the right retroperitoneal and nephrectomy bed without evidence of new nodal recurrence.

5. Incidental cholelithiasis without cholecystitis.

6. Mildly decreased overall tumor burden compared to the prior study, reflecting a favorable response to cabozantinib therapy.

Compared to the scan obtained at the time of documented progression on nivolumab maintenance, there has been a significant reduction in the size of the known pulmonary metastases and pelvic nodal disease, with no new sites of disease. These findings are in keeping with a meaningful treatment response to cabozantinib.
